perturbation.original_text: 1.26. VerticalNet-LeadersOnline Clients shall mean the clients of LeadersOnline that utilize the LeadersOnline Services and pay commissions or other fees to LeadersOnline for the use of its Services, and make initial contact with the LeadersOnline Site through a Link from a VerticalNet Site or through direct access to the LeadersOnline Site if such access was prompted by information presented in a Banner, Newsletter Ad or Employer Spotlight containing information about the LeadersOnline Services.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.26. VerticalNet-LeadersOnline Clients shall mean the clients of LeadersOnline that utilize the LeadersOnline Services and pay commissions or other fees to LeadersOnline for the use of its Services, regardless of how the client initially contacted LeadersOnline.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the condition that VerticalNet-LeadersOnline Clients must make initial contact through a VerticalNet link or promotion, this definition now contradicts Section 6.3.1. It is now unclear on which types of clients LeadersOnline must share revenue with VerticalNet, regardless of whether they have a connection with VerticalNet.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3.1. LeadersOnline shall pay VerticalNet *** percent (***%) of LeadersOnline- VerticalNet Revenue, payable to VerticalNet on or before the fifteenth day of the calendar month immediately following the month in which Source: LEADERSONLINE INC, S-1/A, 4/27/2000 such revenue was received by LeadersOnline. Such payments shall be accompanied by a statement containing reasonable detail of the number of VerticalNet-Leaders Online Clients generated during the month, the type and number of transactions from which the LeadersOnline VerticalNet Revenue was derived, the total LeadersOnline VerticalNet Revenue for such period and the total fees payable to VerticalNet pursuant to this Section 6.3.1 [Revenue Sharing].
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'VerticalNet-LeadersOnline Clients' to include all clients regardless of their initial contact method. This directly contradicts Section 6.3.1, which calculates revenue sharing based on 'VerticalNet-Leaders Online Clients'. The original definition specified that these clients must originate from a VerticalNet source. Now, the contract lacks clarity on which clients LeadersOnline must share revenue with VerticalNet, creating an in-text contradiction that directly impacts the revenue sharing obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 1.13. Link shall mean a link, including but not limited to a hyperlink, button or banner, that connects two Sites in a manner so that when a User clicks on the link, the User is transferred directly from one Site to a second Site.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.13. Link shall mean any reference, whether direct or indirect, that connects two Sites.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Link' is clearly defined as a clickable element that directly transfers a user from one site to another. The changed definition is much broader and includes indirect references. This new definition introduces contradiction in section 2.1 where the term 'VerticalNet Branded Link' is being reffered, now its unclear whether LeadersOnline would be responsible for showing just a clickable link as before or any reference of VerticalNet Site.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1. Hosting and Maintenance. LeadersOnline shall be responsible for the hosting, display and maintenance of the VerticalNet Branded Link on the LeadersOnline Site. The VerticalNet Branded Link shall be located on the Home Page of the LeadersOnline Site and on the first page of each section of the Site, including the sections entitled "Becoming a Member", "Why You Should Join", "How it Works", "About LeadersOnline" and "Recruiter Information". The VerticalNet Branded Link shall be available on each of the foregoing pages as soon as reasonably practicable after the Effective Date, and throughout the Term. It shall be located in an area of the foregoing pages and in a size mutually agreeable to the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of 'Link' broadens its meaning from a clickable element to any reference. This contradicts the original intent of section 2.1, which specifies the 'VerticalNet Branded Link' as a displayable element on the LeadersOnline Site. The change introduces ambiguity as it is now unclear whether LeadersOnline is responsible for merely referencing VerticalNet's Site rather than showing a clickable link, potentially altering their obligation.

perturbation.original_text: 1.1. Affiliate shall mean, when used with reference to a Party, any individual or entity directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by or under common control with such Party. For purposes of this definition, "control" means the direct or indirect ownership of at least 50% of the outstanding voting power of a Party, or the right to control the policy decisions of such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1. Affiliate shall mean, when used with reference to a Party, any individual or entity having a business relationship with such Party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of "Affiliate" to broadly include any entity with a 'business relationship' creates ambiguity. In Section 5.2 [Restrictions], it now becomes unclear whether LeadersOnline must restrict sharing candidate information with entities that merely have a business relationship (e.g., a vendor) or only with those under common control as previously intended. This change causes uncertainty about the scope of restrictions on LeadersOnline's use of the resume database.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2. Restrictions. Any information concerning a Candidate contained in a resume included in the Resume Bank shall not be provided to any third party by LeadersOnline, including corporate Affiliates of LeadersOnline, without the written consent of the individual whose information would be disclosed. LeadersOnline shall limit contact with a Candidate to either one phone contact or one e-mail contact per calendar quarter, unless expressly agreed to by the Candidate. LeadersOnline shall immediately cease all contact upon the request of the Candidate. All information concerning a Candidate, whether in paper or electronic form, and whether originally contained in the Resume Bank or derived from information contained in the Resume Bank, shall be immediately returned to VerticalNet upon the termination of this Agreement. All information contained in the Resume Bank shall be handled by LeadersOnline in accordance with VerticalNet's standard Privacy Policy as it may be posted on the VerticalNet Sites from time to time. The foregoing restrictions shall cease to apply from and after the time that a Candidate registers through an online form or takes other affirmative action to become a member of the LeadersOnline "Candidate Community."
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Affiliate' limited the scope of third parties LeadersOnline was restricted from sharing candidate information with (entities under common control). The changed definition broadens this to any entity with a 'business relationship'. Section 5.2 explicitly restricts sharing candidate information with 'corporate Affiliates of LeadersOnline'. The change creates a contradiction because it is now unclear if LeadersOnline must restrict sharing candidate information with any entity with a mere 'business relationship', which was not intended, thus creating ambiguity and inconsistency with the restrictions outlined in Section 5.2.
